name: Capture stack ScreenCaptureKit and permissions
summary: Modern macOS timelapse should build on ScreenCaptureKit (12.3+) with SCStreamConfiguration.minimumFrameInterval to throttle capture rate (CMTime reciprocal of FPS). macOS 14+ adds SCScreenshotManager for sparse stills; macOS 15+ adds recording output APIs. Production pitfalls: missing com.apple.security.screen-recording entitlement causes silent empty capture outside Xcode; Swift 6 @MainActor constraints for UI; SCK edge cases (3821 errors, sub-window on secondary displays) documented by native recorder builders.
key_findings: minimumFrameInterval CMTime controls max frame rate — e.g. 1 fps = value 1 timescale 1; Entitlement omission fails quietly in signed .app bundles; ScreenSage/FatBobMan documents SCK vs legacy tradeoffs; Recall.ai notes SCK lacks meeting detection — irrelevant for timelapse but shows API scope
implications_for_trylapse: Swift/SwiftUI native menu bar app; Test signed .app from day one; Implement Legacy fallback path if SCK errors; App-scoped capture via SCContentFilter; Document permission flow in onboarding
strengths: Low CPU GPU zero-copy path; Native window/app isolation; System audio on 13+ if needed later
weaknesses: Steep API surface; OS-version gating for features; Silent failures frustrate users
metrics_or_signals: macOS 13+ baseline aligns with TimeLapze and OpenScreen
